title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mercimek Corbasi: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Mercimek Corbasi, a comforting Turkish red lentil soup. This easy recipe brings the taste of Turkey to your kitchen!
longTailKeywords: easy Turkish red lentil soup recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: Turkish red lentil soup from scratch
seo.h1: Authentic Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Step-by-Step: How to Make Mercimek Corbasi
Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Mercimek Corbasi, a traditional Turkish red lentil soup garnished with mint.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of homemade Mercimek Corbasi, the classic Turkish red lentil soup, perfect for any meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and comforting, savory flavor. This hearty and nutritious soup is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a starter or a light meal. Its smooth texture and aromatic spices make it a warming delight, perfect for any time of year.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Vegetables
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Set aside to drain. Chop the onion and carrot, and mince the garlic.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add the dried mint, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine with the vegetables.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils, chicken or vegetable broth, and water to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is smooth and creamy. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint Butter Topping
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the unsalted butter over medium-low heat. Add an additional half teaspoon of dried mint and a pinch of red pepper flakes to the melted butter. Cook for about 1 minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the butter.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with the aromatic mint butter topping. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing into the soup.

tips: For an authentic touch, use Turkish red pepper flakes (Pul Biber) which offer a vibrant color and mild, fruity heat without being overly spicy.
To make this recipe vegan, simply substitute the chicken broth with vegetable broth and use olive oil instead of butter for the topping.
Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. A squeeze of lemon juice brightens the flavors and is a traditional accompaniment.
article:
## Mercimek Corbasi: The Soul-Warming Embrace of Turkish Red Lentil Soup

In the vibrant tapestry of Turkish cuisine, where rich flavors and aromatic spices dance in harmony, few dishes hold as cherished a place as Mercimek Corbasi. This comforting red lentil soup is more than just a meal; it's a culinary hug, a staple found on every Turkish table, from bustling city restaurants to cozy family homes. Whether served as a hearty breakfast, a satisfying lunch, or a warm prelude to an elaborate dinner, Mercimek Corbasi embodies the essence of Turkish hospitality and the simple beauty of wholesome ingredients.

Imagine a bowl of velvety smooth, vibrant orange-red soup, infused with the gentle warmth of mint and chili, and brightened by a squeeze of fresh lemon. This isn't just soup; it's a testament to how humble lentils can be transformed into something truly extraordinary. Easy to prepare, incredibly nutritious, and bursting with flavor, Mercimek Corbasi is a must-try for anyone exploring the delights of Turkish soups and stews.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-suh," literally translates to "lentil soup." While various lentil soups exist across Turkey, the red lentil version is arguably the most iconic and beloved. Its roots run deep within Anatolian culture, where lentils have been a dietary cornerstone for millennia, providing sustenance and protein in an accessible and affordable form.

This soup is characterized by its smooth, creamy texture, achieved by blending the cooked lentils and vegetables until silky. The color typically ranges from a warm orange to a deeper red, depending on the variety of red lentils used and the amount of tomato paste. Unlike some heartier stews, Mercimek Corbasi is typically light yet filling, offering a delicate balance of savory, slightly sweet, and tangy notes.

Its popularity stems from its versatility and universal appeal. It's a dish that transcends seasons – warming in winter, yet light enough for cooler summer evenings. It's often the first dish served in a traditional Turkish meal, awakening the palate with its gentle spices and comforting warmth. For many Turks, the smell of Mercimek Corbasi simmering on the stove evokes memories of home, family, and tradition. It's a soup that nourishes both body and soul.

## Ingredients for Mercimek Corbasi

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its simplicity, relying on a few key, high-quality ingredients to create its signature flavor. You won't find an extensive list of exotic spices here, but rather a thoughtful combination that allows each component to shine.

*   **Red Lentils (Kırmızı Mercimek):** The star of the show. Red lentils cook quickly and break down beautifully, contributing to the soup's creamy texture. No need to pre-soak, just a good rinse.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Finely chopped onion and minced garlic are sautéed to create a sweet and savory base.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** Essential for depth of flavor and that characteristic reddish hue. Turkish tomato paste is often more concentrated and flavorful than Western varieties.
*   **Dried Mint (Kuru Nane):** A non-negotiable ingredient. Dried mint adds a unique, refreshing, and distinctly Turkish aroma that elevates the soup from good to sublime.
*   **Aleppo Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber) or Red Pepper Flakes:** Provides a gentle warmth and a beautiful visual pop, especially in the finishing drizzle. Adjust to your spice preference.
*   **Butter (Tereyağı) or Olive Oil:** Used for sautéing and, crucially, for the final mint-infused drizzle (nane yağı). Butter adds a richer flavor.
*   **Vegetable or Chicken Stock (Et Suyu/Sebze Suyu):** While water can be used, stock adds significant depth and savory notes.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the end brightens all the flavors and is absolutely essential.

Some recipes might include a small amount of flour to thicken, or even a diced carrot or potato for extra body and sweetness, but the core ingredients listed above are the heart of authentic Mercimek Corbasi.

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Mercimek Corbasi

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, even for beginner cooks. Follow these steps to create a truly authentic bowl of this Turkish delight.

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup (about 200g) of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. This step helps to deepen the flavor of the tomato paste. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and ½ teaspoon of red pepper flakes (pul biber) and stir for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
4.  **Combine with Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, along with 6-7 cups (1.5-1.75 liters) of vegetable or chicken stock (or water). Season with 1 teaspoon of salt and ½ teaspoon of black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil.
5.  **Simmer:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer gently for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend:** Remove the pot from the heat. Carefully blend the soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender directly in the pot. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be very careful with hot liquids, filling the blender only halfway and venting the lid) and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot.
7.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot stock or water until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed. Bring it back to a gentle simmer for a few more minutes.
8.  **Prepare the Mint Oil Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** While the soup is simmering, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and foamy, add 1 tablespoon of dried mint and ½ teaspoon of red pepper flakes (pul biber). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm mint-infused butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and crusty bread.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Mercimek Corbasi is easy with a few simple tricks.

*   **Rinse, Don't Soak:** Red lentils cook quickly and don't require soaking. A thorough rinse is sufficient to remove any impurities.
*   **Deepen Tomato Flavor:** Always cook your tomato paste for a minute or two before adding liquids. This caramelizes it slightly and enhances its flavor.
*   **Don't Skip the Mint Oil:** The *nane yağı* (mint-infused butter/oil) is crucial. It adds a burst of fresh mint flavor and a beautiful color contrast that is characteristic of authentic Mercimek Corbasi.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** For that classic velvety texture, ensure your soup is blended completely smooth. Any remaining chunks will detract from the experience.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils can absorb varying amounts of liquid. Don't be afraid to add more hot stock or water at the end to reach your desired consistency – some prefer it thicker, others a bit thinner.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating is non-negotiable. It brightens the flavors and adds a necessary tang that cuts through the richness.
*   **Use Quality Stock:** While water works, a good vegetable or chicken stock significantly enhances the depth of flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Mercimek Corbasi

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways.

*   **Traditional Accompaniments:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges. A generous squeeze of lemon juice stirred into the soup right before eating is essential. Crusty Turkish bread (ekmek) or pita bread is perfect for dipping and soaking up every last drop.
*   **As a Starter:** It makes an excellent appetizer for any Turkish meal, preparing the palate for richer dishes to come.
*   **Light Meal:** Paired with a simple salad, it can be a satisfying and healthy lunch or light dinner.
*   **Garnishes:** Beyond the mint oil, a sprinkle of fresh parsley or finely chopped fresh mint can add an extra layer of freshness and visual appeal. Some also enjoy a spoonful of plain yogurt swirled in, though this is less traditional for red lentil soup.
*   **Alongside Other Dishes:** It pairs well with grilled meats, rice pilafs, or vegetable dishes, offering a light counterpoint to richer flavors.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

One of the great things about Mercimek Corbasi is how easily it can be adapted to various dietary needs and taste preferences.

*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** This soup is naturally vegan if you use vegetable stock and olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and the final drizzle. It’s a fantastic source of plant-based protein.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free. Ensure your stock is also gluten-free, and you have a delicious meal suitable for those avoiding gluten.
*   **Spicier Version:** If you love heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) in both the soup base and the finishing oil. You could also add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** For extra nutrients and a slightly different texture, finely diced carrots, potatoes, or bell peppers can be added along with the onions and garlic, cooked until soft before adding the lentils and liquid.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a lovely depth to the soup, complementing the other spices beautifully.
*   **Herbs:** While dried mint is classic, a touch of dried oregano or thyme can also be added during the sautéing stage for a different aromatic profile.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to experience a cornerstone of Turkish culinary tradition. Its simplicity, comforting warmth, and nourishing qualities make it a dish that will undoubtedly earn a permanent spot in your kitchen repertoire. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be embraced by the heartwarming magic of Turkish red lentil soup.
